framework.name: Fine-Grained Identity Breaks Authorization Policy Model
framework.description: The author identifies but doesn't solve the key problem: if every agent instance gets a unique dynamic identity, you can't write static authorization policies. This is the same structural gap as hop-2 delegation — identity proliferation makes policy unwritable.
framework.raw_quote: if identities are more fine-grained, and even potentially generated on the fly, how can you possibly write authorization policies around this?

source.title: Agent Identity and Access Management - Can SPIFFE Work? | Solo.io
